title: poa cenisia var. halleri
intro: Poa cenisia var. halleri, also known as Alpine meadow-grass, is a subspecies of the Poa cenisia plant, a type of grass native to the European Alps. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The health benefits of Poa cenisia var. halleri include its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as a natural remedy for digestive problems. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in combination with other herbs to promote overall well-being.
